frameworkProfile.language: TypeScript 5.7 (strict), Node 22 (global fetch used by provider impls; no HTTP client dep)
frameworkProfile.projectLayout: src/<feature>/{<feature>.controller.ts,<feature>.service.ts,<feature>.module.ts,tests/<feature>.contract.spec.ts}; shared wire shapes in src/shared (entities.ts, enums.ts, common.ts, dto/*.dto.ts); cross-cutting infra in src/common (guards, filters, decorators, tenancy, concurrency, provider interfaces + impls); src/prisma for the Prisma service. Path aliases @shared and @common (jest moduleNameMapper + tsconfig). Feature modules aggregated in src/app.module.ts in build-wave order.
frameworkProfile.orm: Prisma 6 (prisma-client-js). Datasource sqlite for dev/test (prisma/dev.db), production target PostgreSQL per schema header comment. Migrations: prisma migrate (prisma/migrations: 20260711103511_init, 20260713111325_customer_soft_delete, 20260713140000_model_rev2_metering). Seed: ts-node prisma/seed.ts (5-plan rev 2 catalog: starter|market|business|wholesale|enterprise). Conventions: money = Int kobo, ids = client-minted UUIDv7 strings, enums = strings validated app-side (no Prisma enums), synced entities carry updatedAt + version Int.
frameworkProfile.validation: class-validator decorators on DTO classes in src/shared/dto/*, enforced by a single global ValidationPipe in src/main.ts with { whitelist: true, forbidNonWhitelisted: true, transform: true, enableImplicitConversion: false }. Validation failures are remapped by the global filter from Nest's 400 to 422 VALIDATION_ERROR with the class-validator messages in error.details.
frameworkProfile.authIdiom: Two GLOBAL APP_GUARD providers registered in src/app.module.ts: JwtAuthGuard (passport-jwt bearer strategy 'jwt') then RolesGuard. Routes opt OUT of auth with @Public() (SetMetadata IS_PUBLIC_KEY, checked class+handler). Roles are declared per-route with @Roles('owner'[, 'staff']); RolesGuard lets role 'owner' pass ANY role gate, 'staff' passes only where listed; no @Roles metadata = authenticated-only. Tenancy: @BusinessId() param decorator resolves businessId from the JWT principal (request.user) and throws FORBIDDEN when the claim is null; client-supplied tenancy is never trusted. Optimistic concurrency: 'If-Match: version=N' header parsed by parseIfMatchVersion, checked by assertVersion -> 409 VERSION_CONFLICT with top-level 'current'. An EntitlementsGuard exists (src/common/guards/entitlements.guard.ts) but is a documented NO-OP pass-through and is not registered globally; plan/credit gating is enforced in services (CreditLedgerService, BvumService, StaffService).
frameworkProfile.errorShape: Exactly one envelope: { "error": { "code": <ErrorCode>, "message": string, "details"?: unknown[] }, ...extra } produced ONLY by the global HttpExceptionFilter (src/common/filters/http-exception.filter.ts, registered app.useGlobalFilters in src/main.ts). Services throw AppException subclasses (src/common/exceptions/app.exception.ts) and never hand-roll error JSON. Codes+status (src/shared/enums.ts ERROR_CODE_STATUS): VALIDATION_ERROR 422, UNAUTHENTICATED 401, FORBIDDEN 403, PLAN_REQUIRED 403 (+ error.requiredPlan), BVUM_CEILING 403 (+ error.requiredPlan), NOT_FOUND 404, VERSION_CONFLICT 409 (+ TOP-LEVEL sibling 'current' carrying the current server row), RATE_LIMITED 429, INTERNAL 500. Unmapped statuses: 501 (NotImplementedException from scaffolds) renders as code INTERNAL with HTTP 501.
frameworkProfile.tests: Jest 29 + ts-jest + supertest. Contract specs live at src/<feature>/tests/*.contract.spec.ts (plus provider specs at src/common/providers/impl/tests/ and test/foundation.contract.spec.ts, test/smoke.e2e-spec.ts). Config test/jest-e2e.json, testRegex '.(e2e-spec|contract.spec).ts$'. Authoritative gate: 'npm run test:ci' -> test/run-isolated.sh runs each contract spec file against its OWN fresh SQLite DB (env OWEME_TEST_DB). Style: boot the real Nest app per suite and drive HTTP with supertest against the real Prisma/SQLite stack; external providers resolve to deterministic in-process stubs because provider selection in src/common/common.module.ts is env-credential-driven.
evidence: package.json (nest 11, prisma 6, jest 29, ts-jest, supertest, scripts test:ci/prisma:*)
src/main.ts (global ValidationPipe, global HttpExceptionFilter, rawBody: true for webhook HMAC)
src/app.module.ts (APP_GUARD JwtAuthGuard + RolesGuard, 20 feature modules, ScheduleModule.forRoot() cron transport added by the maintenance pass)
package.json (@nestjs/schedule ^6.1.3 added by the maintenance pass for the reminder dispatch cron)

guards: @Public() — trust boundary is the provider HMAC signature only

headers.x-paystack-signature: string (HMAC-SHA512 of the RAW body with the secret key; required)

request.body: PaystackEvent { event?: string, data?: { reference?: string, amount?: number kobo, metadata?: { debtId?, businessId?, customerId? } } } — provider-defined, only trusted AFTER verification

response.body: $ref WebhookAck. A VERIFIED charge.success is recorded UNCONDITIONALLY in full (charge-recording invariant): archived (deleted=true) debts and overpaying charges are no longer edge cases that skew state silently — the Payment row always carries the full charged amount, remaining clamps to 0, the debt is NEVER unarchived, and a Notification row (kind 'payment') is written per charge: normal -> 'Payment received', archived debt -> 'Archived debt received a payment', overpayment -> 'Debt overpaid' with the excess amount. Settle at paidTotal >= principal clears Debt.nextReminderAt. Idempotent on data.reference

endpoints.uncertain: raw-body fallback still present: when req.rawBody is unavailable the signature is checked over a re-serialization of the parsed body

guards: @Public() — trust boundary is RECEIPT_VERIFIER verification of the signed payload

request.body: IapEvent { platform?: 'ios'|'android', productId?: string, receipt?: string, businessId?: string (present on some provider payloads but NEVER read), notificationType?: string e.g. SUBSCRIBED/DID_RENEW/EXPIRED/CANCELLED }

response.body: $ref WebhookAck. Tenant binding is SERVER-SIDE ONLY: the verified store transaction id is looked up as the BillingTransaction primary key persisted by POST /billing/verify-receipt; no binding -> acked + ignored (processed:false, warn log). Bundle products (oweme_credits_N) are a no-op here (credited only at verify-receipt; re-crediting would double-apply). Plan products apply the subscription lifecycle to the BOUND tenant; expiry types fail the plan CLOSED to starter. The webhook writes NO BillingTransaction rows and NO ledger credits anymore

columns.name: bvumCeilingOverride
columns.note: kobo; rev 2 enterprise banding, sales-provisioned; null -> plan ceiling; NOT exposed on the wire Business shape

schema.deleteSemantics: NO delete path anywhere (no endpoint, no soft-delete column). Tenant root — admin must never hard-delete; every child row FKs to it.

columns.name: role
columns.note: owner|staff; owner unique per business by convention

schema.deleteSemantics: NO delete path. Deactivation only (active=false via PATCH /staff/:id); the owner row can never be deactivated. Staff.id is the JWT sub — deleting a row orphans live sessions and RefreshToken FKs.
HARD delete: deleteMany({phone}) consumes all outstanding codes on successful verify (src/auth/auth.service.ts:87). Expired rows are never purged (no scheduler) — they accumulate.

columns.name: revokedAt
columns.note: soft revocation; non-null revokedAt + reuse -> whole-chain revoke

schema.deleteSemantics: SOFT revoke only (revokedAt set); rows are never deleted. Rotation history is the reuse-detection evidence — do not prune casually.

columns.name: deleted
columns.note: default false; SOFT-DELETE flag; deleted rows surface as sync tombstones

schema.deleteSemantics: SOFT delete (deleted=true + version++), owner-only, CASCADE soft-archives the customer's non-deleted debts in the same transaction (src/customers/customers.service.ts:158-174). No restore endpoint for customers. Admin must mirror: never hard-delete.

columns.name: lastReminderAt
columns.note: written by the reminder dispatch cron on every successful scheduled dispatch (src/reminders/reminder-dispatch.service.ts touchDebt); still untouched by the immediate POST /reminders path

columns.name: nextReminderAt
columns.note: CLEARED on webhook settle; SET/recomputed by the reminder dispatch cron to the earliest still-pending scheduled reminder of the debt (null when none)

schema.deleteSemantics: SOFT delete (deleted=true + version++), owner-only; POST /debts/:id/restore un-archives (any role). Surfaces as sync tombstone. Status/paid/remaining are DERIVED (never stored).

columns.name: reference
columns.note: server-minted OWM-##### or the Paystack charge reference (webhook idempotency key)

schema.deleteSemantics: HARD delete, but ONLY via POST /debts/:id/undo-payment (most-recent payment of a debt). NO soft-delete column -> deletes produce NO sync tombstone (documented v1 limitation, src/sync/sync.service.ts:85-88): offline clients only reconcile via full pull. Admin deletes here would silently desync devices.

NO delete path of any kind and no soft-delete column; sync tombstones structurally empty. Append + status transitions only: scheduled -> sent|failed via the per-minute dispatch cron (atomic updateMany WHERE status='scheduled' claim — count 0 means another tick owns the row, so replicas never double-send/double-debit), failed -> sent via POST /reminders/:id/retry. ADMIN RISK unchanged: flipping a row to 'failed' makes it retryable; flipping a row to 'scheduled' with a past scheduledFor makes the cron send it within a minute.

NO delete path; only read-flag updates (mark-all-read). Rows ARE now created by live code (maintenance pass): the Paystack webhook (kind 'payment') and the reminder dispatch cron (kind 'reminder'). Server-minted UUIDv7 ids.
NO delete path; upsert-only (lazily created on first GET).
NO delete path; upsert-only. Coupled to Business.paystackSubaccount (written together on PUT /payout-account).

columns.name: id
columns.note: @id; canonical ids per seed + enums: starter|market|business|wholesale|enterprise (schema comment fixed by the maintenance pass; no column change)

columns.name: productId
columns.note: IAP product id; join key for verify-receipt/webhook plan routing

columns.name: bvumCeiling
columns.note: kobo; rev 2 seeds concrete values for every tier

schema.deleteSemantics: Seeded reference catalog (prisma/seed.ts, 5 rows); NO runtime writes or deletes. Entitlements, seat caps, credit grants and BVUM ceilings all resolve from it — deleting/renaming a row breaks fail-closed lookups.
NO delete path; upsert-only from billing verify-receipt and the IAP webhook. Server is the SOLE entitlement authority; kept in lockstep with Business.plan (fail-closed to starter on expiry).

columns.name: id
columns.note: @id = STORE TRANSACTION ID — the IAP idempotency key

columns.name: kind
columns.note: LIVE values: 'subscription'|'credits-bundle' (schema comment fixed by the maintenance pass; no column change)

schema.deleteSemantics: Append-only; NO delete or update path. ONLY writer is now POST /billing/verify-receipt (the IAP webhook no longer inserts rows). Triples as (a) IAP idempotency ledger, (b) the counter for the 2-bundles-per-calendar-month hard cap, and (c) the SERVER-SIDE tenant binding the IAP webhook resolves store transactions through — deleting a row severs webhook attribution for that transaction; admin-inserted rows of kind 'credits-bundle' consume a trader's monthly cap.

columns.name: businessId
columns.note: @id — the ONE unified rev 2 'OweMe credits' ledger per business

columns.name: balance
columns.note: default 0; bundle top-ups above the grant carry over month to month

columns.name: periodStart
columns.note: calendar-month UTC period; refill applied LAZILY on read/debit, no scheduler

schema.deleteSemantics: NO delete path; lazily created and refilled by CreditLedgerService.ensure. Weighted debits: reminderSend 5, voiceParse 1, insightOrRisk 4 (latter unwired). Admin credit grants should go through creditCredits semantics (increment), not raw balance writes, to preserve carry-over rules.

otpFlow: src/common/providers/otp-sender.ts + src/common/providers/impl/bulksms-otp-sender.ts (BulkSMS Nigeria v2 SMS, gateway 'otp', MSISDN normalisation)
tokenIssuance: src/auth/auth-token.service.ts (signAccess 15m JWT_ACCESS_SECRET; signRefresh 30d JWT_REFRESH_SECRET + unique jti)
refreshRotation: src/auth/auth.service.ts refresh(): verify sig -> lookup by tokenHash -> revokedAt set means REUSE: revoke user's entire live chain then 401 -> expiry check -> revoke presented token -> issue new pair linked via rotatedFrom. logout() revokes all live tokens for the user.

auth.notes: ENTIRELY protected surface. No SSO; phone + 6-digit OTP via BulkSMSNigeria (stub when BULKSMS_API_TOKEN absent). OTP: hashed at rest, 10-min TTL, max 5 attempts (429), silent throttle 30/phone and 120/ip per rolling minute (always 202). verify-otp auto-provisions a placeholder Business + owner Staff for unknown phones. Refresh tokens stored ONLY as hashes with rotation chain + reuse detection. Secrets: JWT_ACCESS_SECRET / JWT_REFRESH_SECRET with insecure dev fallbacks. Admin auth (per conventions.md) must use a SEPARATE AdminUser table + ADMIN_JWT_SECRET and never touch these files; cross-rejection must be tested both directions.

sideEffects.effect: Creates a Payment row (method 'Paystack link', reference = Paystack charge reference) against metadata.debtId UNCONDITIONALLY and in full — the charge-recording invariant added by the maintenance pass: archived debts and overpayments are recorded, never rejected (the webhook still has NO overpayment guard, by design; remaining derives to 0). Clears Debt.nextReminderAt at paidTotal >= principal. Creates exactly one Notification row (kind 'payment') per charge: 'Payment received', or the anomaly flags 'Archived debt received a payment' (debt stays archived) / 'Debt overpaid' (with the excess). Idempotent on reference. ADMIN RISK: an admin-created Payment carrying a colliding reference suppresses webhook reconciliation AND its owner-facing notification.

sideEffects.trigger: POST /webhooks/iap with a verifier-approved signed payload
sideEffects.effect: Tenant resolved ONLY from the BillingTransaction row keyed by the verified store transaction id (persisted at verify-receipt time); body.businessId is never read; unbound events are acked + ignored with a warn log. Bundle productIds (oweme_credits_N) are a NO-OP here — credits are granted solely at verify-receipt, so webhook retries can never double-apply. Plan productIds -> Business.plan + Subscription upsert on the bound tenant; notificationType in {EXPIRED, CANCELLED, DID_FAIL_TO_RENEW, REVOKE, GRACE_PERIOD_EXPIRED} fails the plan CLOSED to starter. The webhook writes NO BillingTransaction rows and touches NO ledger (WebhooksModule no longer imports UsageModule). ADMIN RISK: deleting a BillingTransaction row severs the tenant binding for future notifications on that transaction.

sideEffects.trigger: Per-minute cron tick (ReminderDispatchService.dispatchDueReminders, @Cron EVERY_MINUTE via @nestjs/schedule; NOT an HTTP endpoint)
sideEffects.effect: Claims up to 100 due rows (status 'scheduled', scheduledFor <= now) with an atomic updateMany WHERE status='scheduled' transition (double-send/double-debit safe across replicas; in-process latch prevents overlapping ticks). sms: debits 5 unified credits BEFORE dispatch via MESSAGE_SENDER; insufficient credits -> 'failed' reason insufficient-credits (nothing debited); provider rejection -> 'failed' reason delivery-failed (NO refund; retry re-debits). whatsapp: always 'failed' reason unsupported-server-channel (no server WhatsApp API; no silent SMS fallback). call|manual|printable: 'sent' free. Every terminal transition bumps Reminder.version, stamps the parent Debt (lastReminderAt on sent, recomputed nextReminderAt, version bump for delta sync) and writes a Notification row (kind 'reminder': 'Reminder sent'/'Reminder failed'). ADMIN RISK: any admin write that sets status 'scheduled' with a past scheduledFor triggers a real SMS + credit debit within a minute.

External IAP verification (Apple verifyReceipt with sandbox/production 21007/21008 retry; Google Play Developer API via service-account JWT). Plan product -> Business.plan + Subscription (30-day renewalAt). Bundle product -> CreditLedger credit, HARD CAP 2 bundle receipts per business per calendar month counted from BillingTransaction rows kind 'credits-bundle'. ADMIN RISK: inserting BillingTransaction rows consumes traders' bundle caps; editing Business.plan out-of-band diverges from Subscription.

Debits 5 OweMe credits from CreditLedger BEFORE dispatching a REAL SMS through MESSAGE_SENDER (BulkSMS Nigeria; the 'whatsapp' channel deliberately falls back to SMS). Exhaustion -> 403 PLAN_REQUIRED with no row/no send. ADMIN RISK: admin credit grants/zeroing instantly change whether trader reminders send; admin flipping a Reminder row to 'failed' would make it retryable (double delivery + double debit).

Creates an OtpCode row and sends a REAL SMS via OTP_SENDER (BulkSMS Nigeria) unless throttled. ADMIN RISK for test-account OTP-viewing tooling: codes are stored HASHED (codeHash) — the planned 'view current OTP' power cannot read codes from this table as-is.
Calls the external LLM (Gemini generateContent, structured JSON output) through LLM_PROVIDER, then debits 1 credit on success only.

Instant BVUM ceiling enforcement reads Plan.bvumCeiling and Business.bvumCeilingOverride. ADMIN RISK: admin plan-force / enterprise-band writes take effect on the very next debt creation (403 BVUM_CEILING appears/disappears immediately).

External payment-gateway transaction initialize (Paystack or Flutterwave per PAYMENT_PROVIDER env) with subaccount split: OweMe 1% (cap N500) as transactionCharge; metadata {debtId, businessId, customerId} is what the webhook later trusts. Disclosed fee is the single combined 2.5%+N100 (cap N2500) figure.
External gateway createSubaccount, then writes PayoutAccount AND Business.paystackSubaccount. All subsequent pay-links split against that subaccount.

sideEffects.trigger: ANY read/debit of the credit ledger (GET /usage, reminder/voice debits, billing balance reads)
sideEffects.effect: Lazy ledger lifecycle: creates the CreditLedger from the plan grant if absent; at calendar-month rollover refills balance to max(current balance, plan grant) — purchased credits carry over; fair-use -1 never meters. Ledger refill has NO scheduler and remains lazy-on-read (@nestjs/schedule IS now installed, but its ONLY consumer is the reminder dispatch cron — it never touches the ledger lifecycle). ADMIN RISK: admin plan changes alter the next refill; raw balance writes bypass carry-over semantics; the dispatch cron debits this same ledger, so zeroing a balance flips pending scheduled reminders to 'failed'.

Real vs deterministic-stub providers chosen SOLELY by env credentials in one place: BULKSMS_API_TOKEN (OTP + messages), PAYMENT_PROVIDER + PAYSTACK_SECRET_KEY / FLUTTERWAVE_SECRET_KEY (gateway incl. webhook signature verification), APPLE_IAP_SHARED_SECRET / GOOGLE_PLAY_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON (IAP), GEMINI_API_KEY (LLM). With keys absent, webhooks/sends/verifies run against stubs.

coverage.routeEnumeration: static — every route is a literal Nest decorator; no dynamic route generation found; maintenance pass added ZERO endpoints
workers: ReminderDispatchService (src/reminders/reminder-dispatch.service.ts) — @Cron EVERY_MINUTE scheduled-reminder delivery; no HTTP route; contract spec src/reminders/tests/reminder-dispatch.contract.spec.ts
